findings.title: handleAdd duplicates server config construction for add vs edit
findings.description: The handleAdd function builds an almost identical config object in both the add and edit branches. The only difference is the edit branch omits 'enabled: true'. The transport-conditional field assignments (command/args/url) are duplicated verbatim, which means any future field changes need to be applied in two places.

findings.title: getStatusColor and getStatusText use if-chains over lookup maps
findings.description: Two functions follow the same pattern: get status, then switch over its three possible values. While each function individually is simple (cyclomatic complexity 3), the duplication of the status-retrieval and branching pattern could be consolidated into a single lookup-based approach. However, the i18n calls for text make a pure lookup slightly awkward, so this is a minor suggestion.

findings.title: recommendedServers computed wraps a pure function with no reactivity
findings.description: getRecommendedServers() returns a static array with no reactive dependencies. Wrapping it in computed() adds reactivity overhead for a value that never changes. A plain const assignment would be simpler and more honest about the data's nature.

positive_observations: All functions are well under the 50-line threshold; the largest (handleAdd) is 27 lines
Maximum nesting depth across all files is 2 levels -- no deep nesting found
Guard clauses and early returns are used consistently (e.g., addFromRecommended, handleAdd validation)
Clean separation: data definitions (recommended-servers.ts), store logic (mcp.ts), and UI (McpManager.vue) are properly isolated
The anima-mcp-server.ts uses dependency injection via the deps parameter rather than hard-coupling to implementations
